name: Button
description: Interactive element for user actions and form submissions

as.description: Render as a native button or anchor element

variant.description: Visual style variant ("icon" is a convenience alias for outlined + icon-only layout)
constraints: Only one primary button per context

icon.description: Render as icon-only button (square aspect ratio). Useful with any visual variant; variant="icon" is the default outlined icon-only shortcut.

fullWidth.description: Make button full width of container

asChild.description: Merge button styling onto child element (e.g. Next.js Link)

when: Triggering an action (save, submit, delete, etc.)
Navigation when action context is needed
whenNot: Selecting from options (use Select or RadioGroup)
guidelines: Use Primary for the main action in a context
Only one Primary button per section/form
Use Danger variant for destructive actions
Loading state should disable the button
When using asChild, pass interaction and accessibility props directly on Button (they are forwarded to the child element)
Use variant="icon" for the default icon-only action button, or combine icon={true} with another visual variant when needed
accessibility: Button text should describe the action

examples.name: Primary
examples.description: Default action button for primary actions

examples.name: Ghost
examples.description: Minimal visual weight for subtle actions

examples.name: Icon
examples.description: Convenience icon-only button alias (ghost + square icon sizing)

examples.name: Sizes
As Child
examples.description: Compose button styles onto another interactive element while preserving forwarded props

relations.note: Use Link for navigation without action context
Use Icon inside Button for icon-leading/trailing or icon-only actions
Use ButtonGroup for related action sets

propsSummary: asChild: boolean - composes styles/props onto a child element (links/router links)
icon: boolean - icon-only square layout (can be combined with visual variants)
